term.empty: No open terminal · click “﹢ New” to start a session
term.hint: Tip: just type (incl. Tab completion / arrow keys / Ctrl+C); Ctrl+V to paste, Shift+PgUp/PgDn to scroll history. Visual effects (hacker palette / CRT / background code) are adjusted on the “Settings” page.
term.session.restartTip: End and restart this terminal (clears session state)
term.subtitle: True pseudo-terminal (ConPTY): supports ssh, interactive prompts, full-screen TUIs (vim / htop, etc.). Open multiple sessions at once; switching tabs never disconnects.
term.surface.exited: [Session ended — press “Restart” to start a new one]
term.surface.notSupported: This system does not support ConPTY (requires Windows 10 1809 or later).
term.title: Terminal
termdlg.close.banner: Disconnecting loses this terminal's entire session state (running processes, command history, current directory, etc.), and it cannot be recovered.
termdlg.close.title: Close terminal session
termdlg.edit.title: Edit terminal session

tweaks.subtitle: One-click toggles for common, reversible system settings (Explorer / appearance / privacy). Most apply to the current user (HKCU) and need no admin; those marked “Admin required” are the exception.

tweaks.toggle.needAdminHint: This item requires running WinDeploy as administrator.
wsl.feature.disabled.body: This machine has wsl.exe, but the “Windows Subsystem for Linux” optional feature is not enabled. It must be enabled before you can download / run distributions:
wsl.feature.disabled.steps:
① Open “Control Panel → Programs → Turn Windows features on or off” (or click the button below)
② Tick “Windows Subsystem for Linux” and “Virtual Machine Platform” (required for WSL2)
③ Confirm and restart the PC
④ Return to this page and click “Refresh detection” to install distributions online
wsl.feature.disabled.title: WSL feature is not yet enabled
wsl.install.hint: (Pick a distribution from the dropdown above, then install)
wsl.install.label: Install distribution:
wsl.msg.enabling: Enabling the WSL feature (allow UAC in the new window, then restart the system and refresh).
wsl.msg.exporting: Exporting {0} … (large distributions may take several minutes)
wsl.msg.featureDisabled: WSL feature is not enabled: first tick WSL under “Control Panel → Programs → Turn Windows features on or off”, then download distributions.
wsl.msg.featureDisabledShort: WSL feature is not enabled: enable WSL in “Turn Windows features on or off” before downloading distributions.
wsl.msg.featureOpened: Opened “Turn Windows features on or off”
wsl.msg.featuresOpenedHint: {0}: tick “Windows Subsystem for Linux” and “Virtual Machine Platform”, confirm, restart, then return to this page and refresh.
wsl.msg.installing: Installing {0} in a new window (click Refresh when done)
wsl.msg.noVmPlatform: (note: “Virtual Machine Platform” is not enabled, so WSL2 is unavailable; you can add it in Windows features)
wsl.note.featureEnabling: Enabling the WSL feature (please allow UAC in the new window; restart the system and refresh when done).
wsl.subtitle: Manage Windows Subsystem for Linux distributions: install, set default, start, stop, export backup (.tar), unregister.
wsl.title: WSL Management
wsl.unregister.confirm:
Unregister and delete the distribution “{0}”?

This deletes all of this distribution's data and cannot be undone!
It is recommended to “Export backup” first.
wsl.unregister.title: Unregister WSL distribution
